message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate three reviewer deliverables for completeness + accuracy. Write /tmp/deliv_gate.md then run 'cd /tmp && timeout 300 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/deliv_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -45' (bubblewrap warning fine; no sandbox flags).
Give codex the three docs' summaries + findings + gaps:
[{"path":"/home/polaris/wt/deliverables/docs/review_readiness/public_compat_inventory.md","summary":"Wrote the Plan V4 Item 2A public-compatibility inventory: an evidence-based catalog of every non-static reference channel in src/polaris_graph that a rename/delete could silently break, cross-referenced against the 210-row RENAME worklist. The load-bearing conclusion is that \"zero static importers\" is insufficient — a symbol can still be reached via string-path dispatch, env-var literals, persisted checkpoint keys, string-keyed config dicts, or external HTTP/CLI names. Six proposed env-var renames REQUIRE aliases; local vars/private helpers/class names (incl. ResearchStateV2) are SAFE.","key_findings":["STAGE_TYPE_REGISTRY (pipeline_definition.py:47-59) stores 11 module paths as STRINGS (e.g. \"src.polaris_graph.agents.planner\"); grep-for-import and IDE rename miss them entirely. Confirmed the ONLY string-literal module paths in the package, and confirmed it currently has NO runtime consumer (grep STAGE_TYPE_REGISTRY returns only its own definition) — safe only because it is dead, a latent trap once the pipeline compiler is wired.","Env-var control surface is the largest risk: 839 PG_* keys in config_defaults.py, 1592 distinct PG_* reads. Six worklist RENAMEs hit live env reads and REQUIRE aliases: PG_V30_ENABLED (honest_sweep_integration.py:92,96), PG_V2_ENABLED (scripts/live_server.py:560), PG_JUNK_SOURCE_SCREEN and PG_CONTRADICTION_RENDER_HONEST (kill-switches in run_honest_sweep_r3.py:1280,4856), PG_S15_CORROBORATED_HONEST_LABEL (string dict keys in run_gate_b.py x4), PG_LETHAL_SEED_K (config_defaults.py:390='80' + resolve() at lethal.py:50). A naive rename makes operator settings silently no-op.","Saved-state hazards: LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver persists TypedDict FIELD keys (registry_data/entries/counter, graph_v2.py:96,941,950) and graph NODE-NAME strings (\"plan\",\"search\",...,\"v3_*\" in graph.py:1242-1274, graph_v2.py:630-676, graph_v3.py:680-703), plus a pg_{vector_id} thread-id prefix (checkpoint_manager.py:55). These orphan on rename. But the ResearchStateV2 CLASS name is NOT persisted (serialized by field) — so renaming the class is SAFE.","No \"cls\":\"Name\" / __class__.__name__ class-name serialization anywhere; no pickle (snapshots explicitly say 'No pickle'); no [project.scripts] entry points. External surface = FastAPI route path literals (graph_route.py:250, intake_route.py:66, audit_bundle_route.py:169, etc.), wire Literal[...] value sets (graph_route.py:37-40 Tier/NodeType, schema_version \"1.0\"), and ~80 scripts/*.py filenames operators/CI invoke directly.","SAFE-to-rename bulk: the ~80 in-package RENAME rows that are function-local vars / module-private helpers in live_retriever.py (_jo_doi, _w5_loop_idx, _mv_now, _row0 at lines 7142-8150), generator/* private funcs, and benchmark/* BEAT-BOTH->head-to-head docstring cleanups — static references only."],"gaps_or_risks":["Worklist TSV is NOT in the deliverables worktree; it was read from the sibling checkout /home/polaris/polaris_project/NAME_RENAME_WORKLIST_validated.tsv (same package, 346 rows). Line numbers in the worklist's LOCATION column were taken as-is and not all re-verified against this worktree's current line numbers — a few worklist rows point at scripts/ files whose line numbers may have drifted.","STAGE_TYPE_REGISTRY is currently dead (no importlib consumer found), so I classified it 'safe only because dead.' If a pipeline-editor compiler exists in the web/ frontend or another repo/worktree not scanned here, it could consume those module-path strings at runtime; I only grepped src/, scripts/, tests/, web/ within this worktree.","The env-var alias recommendation assumes no existing alias shim. I confirmed the six keys are absent from config_defaults.py (except PG_LETHAL_SEED_K) but did not exhaustively prove no compatibility shim exists elsewhere; implementer should grep for the old names once more before adding aliases.","Checkpoint persistence is gated behind PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED (default '0'), so the saved-state hazards only bite operators who enabled it and have on-disk state/pg_checkpoints.sqlite; the doc states this but the real-world blast radius depends on deployment configs I could not inspect.","I did not enumerate all 210 RENAME rows individually — I filtered to the 87 touching src/polaris_graph plus the env-key rows and sampled the scripts/ file renames. A row touching a dynamic surface with an unusual LOCATION string could have been missed by my awk filters."]},{"path":"/home/polaris/wt/deliverables/docs/review_readiness/threat_model.md","summary":"Wrote the Plan V4 S4 security/privacy threat model to /home/polaris/wt/deliverables/docs/review_readiness/threat_model.md, covering all six required axes (API auth, crawler SSRF, prompt injection, PII, log redaction, checkpoint retention) with file/function/line citations, a consolidated severity table, and a clearly flagged UNRESOLVED CRITICAL. The dominant finding is crawler SSRF: arbitrary and LLM-harvested URLs are fetched verbatim with follow_redirects=True and zero private-IP/metadata-address filtering anywhere in the fetch path. Production auth (polaris_v6) is coherent and fail-loud (global JWT dependency, bcrypt logins, POLARIS_JWT_SECRET >=32-char startup check), but two dormant auth systems exist, one (src/auth/) with insecure defaults. Prompt injection has a real per-call-site delimiter/sanitization defense but no single choke point and no output validation. No PII redaction exists. Log secret hygiene is good (keys never logged as values; dedicated secret scanner).","key_findings":["CRITICAL/UNRESOLVED SSRF: _fetch_content_httpx_naive (live_retriever.py:2999-3025) and other fetchers (domain_backends.py:211,246; access_bypass.py:5590; utils/ingest.py:1959) fetch caller/LLM-supplied URLs with follow_redirects=True and NO private-IP/loopback/169.254 metadata filtering. agentic_url_harvester.py:8 documents that LLM-harvested seed URLs are fetched VERBATIM. Grep for is_private/ip_address/169.254/gethostbyname across src/ returns zero hits in any fetch path.","Production API auth (src/polaris_v6/api/app.py:71-79) is sound: global Depends(_require_auth) HS256 JWT dependency, bcrypt static-accounts login, fail-loud verify_app_startup() requiring POLARIS_JWT_SECRET >=32 chars (auth.py:88-100), public allowlist limited to /health,/transparency,/auth/login,/docs. Secrets sourced from AWS Secrets Manager, .env gitignored.","Latent auth risk: src/auth/ HMAC system (auth_manager.py:16-17,135-159) has insecure defaults (POLARIS_AUTH_ENABLED off by default, hardcoded 'polaris-dev-secret-change-in-production', default admin/admin, unsalted-ish SHA-256 passwords) but is NOT imported by the served app. A third system (audit_ir API-key RBAC) is also deliberately not mounted (inspector.py:15-19). Single-env kill switch POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 disables all auth (auth.py:104-107).","Prompt injection HAS a real defense (per-call random-token delimiters build_question_block scope_classifier_llm.py:262-276, evidence-text sanitization analyst_synthesis.py:159,407-424, fail-loud if sanitizer disabled outline_digest.py:88-101) but it is per-call-site not a single boundary, and there is no output-side validation; chains into the SSRF finding.","No PII redaction anywhere (grep redact/pii/anonymiz empty); query+document+retrieved text sent verbatim to third-party providers. The only scrubber (cot_scrubber.py) removes chain-of-thought from reports, not PII. Log secret hygiene is good: OpenRouter key only in Authorization header not logs (openrouter_client.py:1563-1587), search_agent.py:492 logs only bool(present), dedicated scan_for_secrets.py commit scanner.","Checkpoint retention: LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver (checkpoint_manager.py:26-30) persists full state incl. drafts+retrieved content unencrypted at rest; only cleanup is manual per-vector clear_checkpoint (lines 80-112); no TTL/prune/purge/age-based retention exists."],"gaps_or_risks":["I confirmed src/auth/ is not imported by the v6 served app via grep, but did not exhaustively trace every alternative entrypoint (e.g. scripts, Pipeline-B legacy servers referenced in .env.example POLARIS_AUTH_ENABLED). If a separate legacy server binary mounts src/auth routes, its insecure defaults would be live there.","The SSRF assessment is based on static grep + reading the primary fetch functions; I did not runtime-test an actual 169.254 fetch. The absence of any is_private/ip_address guard across src/ is strong evidence, but a defense could theoretically live in an httpx transport/proxy layer or network egress firewall not visible in code (none found).","I did not fully verify whether any fetch logs the full parameterized API URL (which could carry NCBI/S2 keys as query params) at INFO level; live_retriever truncates url[:80] but other backends were not exhaustively checked, so log-leak of key-bearing URLs is possible-but-unconfirmed.","Prompt-injection coverage claim (per-call-site, not centralized) is inferred from the sanitizer living in specific modules; I did not enumerate every prompt-construction call site to prove a gap exists, so the exact number of unsanitized paths is unquantified.","Checkpoint encryption-at-rest: I confirmed the app layer applies none, but did not verify the deployment volume (docker/helm) is or isn't an encrypted disk; the residual-risk rating assumes plaintext host storage."]},{"summary":"Wrote an evidence-based OPERATIONAL READINESS checklist (Plan V4 item S5) at /home/polaris/wt/deliverables/docs/review_readiness/operational_readiness.md. Inventoried the src/polaris_graph deep-research pipeline across timeouts, retries, rate/cost limits, monitoring, runbooks, and recovery, with concrete file:line evidence and Present/Partial/Missing marks. Verdict: operationally mature for a supervised single-host demo, but not yet hardened for unattended external exposure. Analysis + new doc only; no runtime code modified.","path":"/home/polaris/wt/deliverables/docs/review_readiness/operational_readiness.md","key_findings":["TIMEOUTS = Present: 58 *_TIMEOUT knobs in config_defaults.py; whole-run wall clock at graph_v3.py:791-796; reasoning-first-aware per-call sizing at openrouter_client.py:901-935; audit for bare requests/httpx/aiohttp/urlopen calls WITHOUT timeout returned zero hits (bound is per-callsite, not structural).","RETRIES = Present (hand-rolled, no tenacity): openrouter_client.py:936-938/2161-2420 (MAX_RETRIES, 429 backoff floor 15/30/60s), _parse_retry_after at :1018-1049; fetch_limiter.py:74-130 (exp backoff+jitter); frame_fetcher.py:686-702; planner.py:425-462; verifier.py:491-579.","COST = Present/strong: persistent JSONL ledger (PG_COST_LEDGER_PATH), context-scoped per-task accounting (contextvars) surviving concurrent gather(), hard per-run guard check_run_budget/BudgetExceededError (PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN default 10.00) that propagates not masks. CONCURRENCY = Present: 19 *_CONCURRENCY knobs + shared semaphores (llm_throttle.py, fetch_limiter.py).","RATE LIMITING = Partial: in-process best-effort only (S2 <=1rps time.sleep at live_retriever.py:6948, 429 floors, Exa Retry-After). NO shared/distributed limiter across worker replicas -> aggregate provider QPS can exceed limits under docker-compose.v6.yml multi-worker.","MONITORING = mixed: tracing Present (tracing.py JSONL, PG_TRACING_ENABLED default 1); logging Partial (basicConfig only in graph.py:2223, no dictConfig/JSON logger for library callers); metrics MISSING (no prometheus/otel/statsd, grep=0); health checks Partial (polaris_v6/api/health.py:16-18 static liveness only, no dependency/readiness check).","RECOVERY = Partial: checkpoint_manager.py exists but PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED defaults 0 (ships OFF); resume_refetch.py idempotent re-fetch default OFF; graceful degradation Present (OpenAlex->S2->DDG fallbacks, provider exclusion on stall) with deliberate fail-closed core retrieval posture.","RUNBOOKS = Present: docs/runbook.md (433 lines incl. actionable §8 failure modes mapping abort codes to env-var remediations), deploy_runbook.md, carney_demo_runbook.md, verifier_serving_runbook.md; but all demo/single-host scoped, no production incident/on-call playbook."],"gaps_or_risks":["Repo contains two packages: src/polaris_graph (in-scope pipeline) and src/polaris_v6 (serving). The only real /health endpoint and Docker HEALTHCHECK live in polaris_v6, not in the polaris_graph library itself; a reviewer should confirm which package is actually the external-exposure surface.","PG_S2_RPS=1.0 exists in config_defaults.py:657 but I did not find it wired into an actual cross-worker limiter; the observed S2 throttle is a hardcoded time.sleep(0.2) every 5 items in live_retriever.py, so the RPS knob may be dead config.","'All external calls bounded' is asserted from a grep that found no bare timeout-less calls in sampled patterns; multiline call forms and dynamically-constructed clients were not exhaustively proven, so the claim is marked Partial rather than Present.","Default values (PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN=10.00, PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED=0, PG_TRACING_ENABLED=1) are read from config_defaults.py; actual deployed values could be overridden by .env/compose and were not verified against a running deployment.","The openrouter_client.py file is 208KB / ~3900 lines; retry and cost logic were sampled at the cited line ranges, not read end-to-end, so edge-case retry branches beyond the cited lines are not fully audited."]}]
Ask codex: "These are Plan V4 deliverables 2A (public-compat inventory), S4 (security threat model), S5 (operational readiness) for a deep-research pipeline. For EACH: is it complete enough to hand an independent reviewer, or does it have a material gap/inaccuracy? Name the single most important thing each is missing. Flag any security finding that is understated. End with one line per doc: 2A: OK|REVISE, S4: OK|REVISE, S5: OK|REVISE."
Return codex's per-doc verdicts + its key points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll write the gate prompt and run CODEX to evaluate the three deliverables.

input.content:
You are gating three reviewer deliverables for a deep-research pipeline (Python, src/polaris_graph library + src/polaris_v6 serving layer). These are Plan V4 deliverables:

- 2A: public-compatibility inventory (what a rename/delete could silently break)
- S4: security/privacy threat model
- S5: operational readiness checklist

I will give you each doc's own SUMMARY, KEY FINDINGS, and GAPS/RISKS (self-declared by the author). Evaluate whether each is complete enough to hand to an independent reviewer, or whether it has a material gap or inaccuracy.

====================================================================
DOC 2A — public_compat_inventory.md
--------------------------------------------------------------------
SUMMARY:
Plan V4 Item 2A public-compatibility inventory: an evidence-based catalog of every non-static reference channel in src/polaris_graph that a rename/delete could silently break, cross-referenced against a 210-row RENAME worklist. Load-bearing conclusion: "zero static importers" is insufficient — a symbol can still be reached via string-path dispatch, env-var literals, persisted checkpoint keys, string-keyed config dicts, or external HTTP/CLI names. Six proposed env-var renames REQUIRE aliases; local vars/private helpers/class names (incl. ResearchStateV2) are SAFE.

KEY FINDINGS:
1. STAGE_TYPE_REGISTRY (pipeline_definition.py:47-59) stores 11 module paths as STRINGS (e.g. "src.polaris_graph.agents.planner"); grep-for-import and IDE rename miss them. Confirmed the ONLY string-literal module paths in the package, and confirmed it currently has NO runtime consumer (grep returns only its own definition) — safe only because it is dead, a latent trap once the pipeline compiler is wired.
2. Env-var control surface is the largest risk: 839 PG_* keys in config_defaults.py, 1592 distinct PG_* reads. Six worklist RENAMEs hit live env reads and REQUIRE aliases: PG_V30_ENABLED, PG_V2_ENABLED, PG_JUNK_SOURCE_SCREEN, PG_CONTRADICTION_RENDER_HONEST, PG_S15_CORROBORATED_HONEST_LABEL, PG_LETHAL_SEED_K. A naive rename makes operator settings silently no-op.
3. Saved-state hazards: LangGraph AsyncSqliteSaver persists TypedDict FIELD keys and graph NODE-NAME strings, plus a pg_{vector_id} thread-id prefix. These orphan on rename. But the ResearchStateV2 CLASS name is NOT persisted (serialized by field) — renaming the class is SAFE.
4. No "cls":"Name"/__class__.__name__ class-name serialization anywhere; no pickle; no [project.scripts] entry points. External surface = FastAPI route path literals, wire Literal[...] value sets, ~80 scripts/*.py filenames operators/CI invoke directly.
5. SAFE-to-rename bulk: ~80 in-package RENAME rows that are function-local vars / module-private helpers, static references only.

GAPS/RISKS (self-declared):
- Worklist TSV is NOT in the deliverables worktree; read from a sibling checkout (346 rows). Line numbers taken as-is, not all re-verified against this worktree; some scripts/ rows may have drifted.
- STAGE_TYPE_REGISTRY currently dead (no importlib consumer found); if a pipeline-editor compiler exists in web/ frontend or another repo/worktree not scanned, it could consume those strings at runtime; only grepped src/scripts/tests/web within this worktree.
- Env-var alias recommendation assumes no existing alias shim; confirmed six keys absent from config_defaults.py (except PG_LETHAL_SEED_K) but did not exhaustively prove no shim exists elsewhere.
- Checkpoint persistence gated behind PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED (default 0), so saved-state hazards only bite operators who enabled it and have on-disk state.
- Did not enumerate all 210 RENAME rows individually — filtered to 87 touching src/polaris_graph plus env-key rows and sampled scripts/ renames.

====================================================================
DOC S4 — threat_model.md
--------------------------------------------------------------------
SUMMARY:
Plan V4 S4 security/privacy threat model covering six axes (API auth, crawler SSRF, prompt injection, PII, log redaction, checkpoint retention) with file/function/line citations, a consolidated severity table, and a flagged UNRESOLVED CRITICAL. Dominant finding: crawler SSRF — arbitrary and LLM-harvested URLs fetched verbatim with follow_redirects=True and zero private-IP/metadata-address filtering. Production auth (polaris_v6) coherent and fail-loud. Two dormant auth systems exist, one (src/auth/) with insecure defaults. Prompt injection has per-call-site delimiter/sanitization defense but no single choke point and no output validation. No PII redaction. Log secret hygiene good.

KEY FINDINGS:
1. CRITICAL/UNRESOLVED SSRF: _fetch_content_httpx_naive (live_retriever.py:2999-3025) and other fetchers fetch caller/LLM-supplied URLs with follow_redirects=True and NO private-IP/loopback/169.254 metadata filtering. LLM-harvested seed URLs fetched VERBATIM. Grep for is_private/ip_address/169.254/gethostbyname across src/ returns zero hits in any fetch path.
2. Production API auth (polaris_v6/api/app.py:71-79) sound: global Depends(_require_auth) HS256 JWT, bcrypt login, fail-loud verify_app_startup() requiring POLARIS_JWT_SECRET >=32 chars, public allowlist limited to /health,/transparency,/auth/login,/docs. Secrets from AWS Secrets Manager, .env gitignored.
3. Latent auth risk: src/auth/ HMAC system has insecure defaults (off by default, hardcoded 'polaris-dev-secret-change-in-production', default admin/admin, unsalted-ish SHA-256 passwords) but NOT imported by served app. Third system (audit_ir API-key RBAC) deliberately not mounted. Single-env kill switch POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 disables all auth.
4. Prompt injection HAS a real defense (per-call random-token delimiters, evidence-text sanitization, fail-loud if sanitizer disabled) but per-call-site not a single boundary, no output-side validation; chains into SSRF.
5. No PII redaction anywhere; query+document+retrieved text sent verbatim to third-party providers. Only scrubber removes chain-of-thought from reports, not PII. Log secret hygiene good (key only in Authorization header not logs; dedicated commit scanner).
6. Checkpoint retention: AsyncSqliteSaver persists full state incl. drafts+retrieved content unencrypted at rest; only cleanup is manual per-vector clear_checkpoint; no TTL/prune/purge/age-based retention.

GAPS/RISKS (self-declared):
- Confirmed src/auth/ not imported by v6 app via grep, but did not exhaustively trace every alternative entrypoint (scripts, Pipeline-B legacy servers). If a separate legacy server binary mounts src/auth routes, its insecure defaults would be live there.
- SSRF assessment based on static grep + reading primary fetch functions; did not runtime-test a 169.254 fetch. A defense could theoretically live in an httpx transport/proxy layer or egress firewall not visible in code (none found).
- Did not fully verify whether any fetch logs the full parameterized API URL (which could carry NCBI/S2 keys as query params) at INFO level; log-leak of key-bearing URLs possible-but-unconfirmed.
- Prompt-injection coverage claim (per-call-site, not centralized) inferred; did not enumerate every prompt-construction call site, so exact number of unsanitized paths unquantified.
- Checkpoint encryption-at-rest: confirmed app layer applies none, but did not verify deployment volume is/isn't encrypted disk.

====================================================================
DOC S5 — operational_readiness.md
--------------------------------------------------------------------
SUMMARY:
Plan V4 S5 operational readiness checklist. Inventoried the src/polaris_graph deep-research pipeline across timeouts, retries, rate/cost limits, monitoring, runbooks, recovery with file:line evidence and Present/Partial/Missing marks. Verdict: operationally mature for a supervised single-host demo, but not hardened for unattended external exposure.

KEY FINDINGS:
1. TIMEOUTS = Present: 58 *_TIMEOUT knobs; whole-run wall clock; reasoning-first-aware per-call sizing; audit for bare requests/httpx/aiohttp/urlopen calls WITHOUT timeout returned zero hits (bound per-callsite, not structural).
2. RETRIES = Present (hand-rolled, no tenacity): MAX_RETRIES, 429 backoff floor 15/30/60s, _parse_retry_after; fetch_limiter exp backoff+jitter; frame_fetcher; planner; verifier.
3. COST = Present/strong: persistent JSONL ledger, context-scoped per-task accounting (contextvars) surviving concurrent gather(), hard per-run guard check_run_budget/BudgetExceededError (PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN default 10.00) that propagates not masks. CONCURRENCY = Present: 19 *_CONCURRENCY knobs + shared semaphores.
4. RATE LIMITING = Partial: in-process best-effort only (S2 <=1rps time.sleep, 429 floors, Exa Retry-After). NO shared/distributed limiter across worker replicas -> aggregate provider QPS can exceed limits under multi-worker compose.
5. MONITORING = mixed: tracing Present (JSONL, default on); logging Partial (basicConfig only, no dictConfig/JSON logger for library callers); metrics MISSING (no prometheus/otel/statsd); health checks Partial (static liveness only, no dependency/readiness check).
6. RECOVERY = Partial: checkpoint_manager exists but PG_CHECKPOINT_ENABLED defaults 0 (ships OFF); resume_refetch idempotent re-fetch default OFF; graceful degradation Present (OpenAlex->S2->DDG fallbacks) with deliberate fail-closed core retrieval posture.
7. RUNBOOKS = Present: runbook.md (433 lines incl. actionable failure modes mapping abort codes to env-var remediations), deploy_runbook.md, others; but all demo/single-host scoped, no production incident/on-call playbook.

GAPS/RISKS (self-declared):
- Repo contains two packages: src/polaris_graph (in-scope pipeline) and src/polaris_v6 (serving). The only real /health endpoint and Docker HEALTHCHECK live in polaris_v6, not polaris_graph; reviewer should confirm which package is the external-exposure surface.
- PG_S2_RPS=1.0 exists but not found wired into an actual cross-worker limiter; observed S2 throttle is hardcoded time.sleep every 5 items, so the RPS knob may be dead config.
- 'All external calls bounded' asserted from grep that found no bare timeout-less calls in sampled patterns; multiline call forms and dynamically-constructed clients not exhaustively proven, so claim marked Partial.
- Default values read from config_defaults.py; actual deployed values could be overridden by .env/compose, not verified against a running deployment.
- openrouter_client.py is 208KB/~3900 lines; retry and cost logic sampled at cited line ranges, not read end-to-end.

- **2A — Materially incomplete.** The missing item is a row-by-row disposition of the authoritative 210-row worklist, using the same revision. An unavailable 346-row sibling TSV plus sampled rows is not reproducible. Also, `ResearchStateV2` being checkpoint-safe does **not** make its public import name safe for external consumers; that SAFE conclusion needs export/API evidence or a compatibility alias.

- **S4 — Materially incomplete.** The missing item is an end-to-end reachability/trust-boundary analysis: which authenticated or unauthenticated inputs reach each fetcher, redirect and DNS-rebinding behavior, network access, and whether internal responses are observable. SSRF at **Critical** is reasonable if reachable from an exposed API and internal/metadata access is possible; otherwise High pending deployment validation. `POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1` should itself be rated at least High if production startup permits it, so “production auth sound/fail-loud” is overstated. Verbatim PII egress to third parties also needs provider-retention and consent/compliance treatment.

- **S5 — Materially incomplete.** The missing item is an end-to-end operational assessment of `src/polaris_v6` and its deployment boundary. The stated scope includes serving, yet request admission, server timeouts, backpressure, graceful shutdown, dependency readiness, SLOs/alerts, and multi-worker behavior remain unassessed. “TIMEOUTS = Present” also conflicts with the acknowledged sampled/Partial evidence.

content.text: CODEX returned all three verdicts. Returning them via structured output.

codex_points: 2A — REVISE (materially incomplete). Single most important missing item: a row-by-row disposition of the AUTHORITATIVE 210-row worklist at the same revision. An unavailable 346-row sibling TSV plus sampled rows is not reproducible for an independent reviewer.

2A — Inaccuracy flagged: ResearchStateV2 being checkpoint-safe does NOT make its PUBLIC IMPORT NAME safe for external consumers. The SAFE conclusion needs export/API evidence or a compatibility alias; author conflated serialization-safety with import-surface-safety.

S4 — REVISE (materially incomplete). Single most important missing item: an end-to-end reachability/trust-boundary analysis — which authenticated/unauthenticated inputs reach each fetcher, redirect + DNS-rebinding behavior, network access, and whether internal responses are observable.

S4 — SSRF severity: Critical is REASONABLE only IF reachable from an exposed API and internal/metadata access is possible; otherwise it is High pending deployment validation. Note: DNS-rebinding is an additional bypass vector not covered even if a private-IP filter were added.

S4 — Understated finding: POLARIS_AUTH_DISABLED=1 kill switch should itself be rated at least HIGH if production startup permits it. Therefore the 'production auth sound / fail-loud' characterization is OVERSTATED.
S4 — Understated finding: verbatim PII egress to third-party providers needs provider-retention and consent/compliance treatment, not just a 'no redaction' note.

S5 — REVISE (materially incomplete). Single most important missing item: an end-to-end operational assessment of src/polaris_v6 and its deployment boundary. Stated scope includes serving, yet request admission, server timeouts, backpressure, graceful shutdown, dependency readiness, SLOs/alerts, and multi-worker behavior are unassessed.

S5 — Inaccuracy flagged: 'TIMEOUTS = Present' conflicts with the author's own acknowledged sampled/Partial evidence (grep-based, multiline/dynamic clients not exhaustively proven) — should be marked Partial for consistency.

Overall: all three docs are self-honest about gaps but each has a material scope hole (2A: non-reproducible worklist basis; S4: no trust-boundary/reachability model; S5: serving layer polaris_v6 unassessed) that blocks handing to an independent reviewer as-is.
